─[ OpenCode session.created Not a Hook ]─ ┐

name: opencode-session-created-not-a-hook description: OpenCode session.created is a bus event, not a plugin hook — use system.transform instead user-invocable: false allowed-tools: Read, Grep

OpenCode session.created Not a Hook

OpenCode publishes session.created on its internal event bus, and the docs list it under “session events.” But it is not exposed as a plugin hook in the Hooks interface.

Registering session.created in a plugin’s return object compiles fine and loads without error — OpenCode silently ignores unknown hook names. The plugin appears healthy in logs but the handler never fires.

Snags

  • session.created appears in OpenCode logs (service=bus type=session.created publishing) but is never dispatched to plugins.
  • The plugin SDK types (@opencode-ai/plugin) do not include session.created in the Hooks interface — check the types before assuming a hook exists.
  • No error, no warning — the plugin loads, initialization code runs, but the hook callback is dead code.

Fix

Use experimental.chat.system.transform to inject context into the system prompt:

return {
  'experimental.chat.system.transform': async (_input, output) => {
    output.system.push(myContent);
  },
};

This runs on every LLM call. The full banner injects at session start and after compaction; subsequent calls inject a compact one-line reminder.

Valid Plugin Hooks (as of SDK 1.2.6)

event, config, tool, auth, chat.message, chat.params, chat.headers, permission.ask, command.execute.before, tool.execute.before, tool.execute.after, shell.env, experimental.chat.system.transform, experimental.chat.messages.transform, experimental.session.compacting, experimental.text.complete, tool.definition

PgUp/PgDn: scroll b: bulk commands q: quit x: menu ← → ↑ ↓: navigate
Donate 0.24.4
